story: Maeve, Ballycarra’s Keeper of Fish, listens to villagers’ sorrows, whispers them into the well, then each season nets the fish shaped from those memories and carries them to the sea. At year’s end, she names each and lets it fall to the waves. Last, a copper fish looks back: Liam, her first love, long believed drowned. She wades in and opens her hands; two lights fold into the current and are gone. Beyond the headland, fishermen see the barometer plunge and raise a storm flag; a squall will soon slam the rocks and drown the low path. Maeve lingers with her empty basket, the tide tugging at her feet. Rain begins, soft and steady; village lights glow dim through fog.
implausible_knowledge: Beyond the headland, fishermen see the barometer plunge and raise a storm flag; a squall will soon slam the rocks and drown the low path.
question: As the soft rain begins and the tide tugs at her feet, what will Maeve most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Leave the rocks at once and take the higher path home before the waves cut it off.
answer_without_knowledge: Linger by the shore a while longer, letting the rain fall before walking back along the rocks.
